title: lesquerella stenophylla
intro: Lesquerella stenophylla, also known as Wyoming globe mallow, is a perennial plant species native to North America. It belongs to the Brassicaceae family and is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. The plant's seeds and oil have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues, offering benefits like reduced inflammation, improved skin health, and relief from congestion. Herbal preparations of Lesquerella stenophylla include oil extracts, salves, and infusions, which can be used topically or internally, depending on the desired application.
